data.title: Burford Capital Statement Re YPF Matter
data.description: On March 27, 2026, Burford Capital Limited disclosed that the US Court of Appeals for the Second Circuit rendered its verdict on Burford's Petersen and Eton cases. The Second Circuit's decision holds significance for the leading global finance and asset management firm specializing in law. The details of the ruling are pivotal for Burford, indicating a potentially substantial impact on their operations and future strategies. This development marks a crucial moment for Burford as it navigates the legal landscape and enhances its position in the industry.

data.title: Wecom Fiber Appoints Rachel Solomon Chief Executive Officer to Drive Operational Execution and Expansion; Paul Fleming Transitions to Chairman, Leading Government and External Affairs
data.description: On March 23, 2026, Wecom Fiber appointed Rachel Solomon as its new Chief Executive Officer. Paul Fleming, the current CEO, will move to the role of Chairman of the Board and Head of Government & External Affairs. This leadership transition comes amidst a changing landscape for the company and marks a shift in focus towards governmental relations.

data.title: Kaldalón hf.: Implementation of a Share Buyback Programme
data.description: At the annual general meeting held on March 26, 2026, Kaldalón hf. (“Kaldalón” or the “Company”) received approval for its Board of Directors to purchase shares in the Company, allowing them to hold up to 10% of the share capital, in line with legal requirements. The primary objective of this authorization is to facilitate market making in the Company’s shares and potentially initiate a formal share buyback program, adhering to Regulation (EU) No. 596/2014 and Commission Delegated Regulation (EU) 2016/1052, as well as Act No. 60/2021 on Measures Against Market Abuse. This authorization, granted at the annual general meeting, remains valid until June 30, 2027.

data.title: Cryptocurrency market sees Bitcoin drop to lowest since March 2, affecting Strategy, BitMine, and Robinhood shares.
data.description: On Friday, the price of Bitcoin dropped to its lowest point since March 2nd, while prominent crypto stocks such as Strategy and BitMine experienced significant declines.
